title: senecio eremophilus var. macdougalii
intro: Senecio eremophilus var. macdougalii, also known as a variety of the upright Jerusalem sage, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its saponins and alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, and it is also believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared for herbal use through infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
